record.paper_title: Joint Model and Data Sparsification via the Marginal Likelihood

claims.claim: Joint ARD extends standard Automatic Relevance Determination by introducing per-sample noise variances lambda_i as primary parameters, yielding a heteroscedastic marginal-likelihood objective that jointly sparsifies features and down-weights outlying samples.
The heteroscedastic objective admits closed-form updates across EM, MacKay, l1-IRLS, and l2-IRLS schemes while preserving conjugacy.
Table 3 quantitative RMSE reductions and ESS behavior.
Figure 4 real-data RVM robustness and Figure 3 sparse/low-contamination gain regime.
